steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: 'comprises some 1,200 languages [BLANK] including the Nauruan and Marshallese...' The blank asks what punctuation, if any, follows 'languages.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘languages—’: would set off what follows as a dramatic aside, but 'including' as a plain modifier does not call for that emphasis and creates an unmatched dash. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘languages:’: requires a complete statement before it; 'comprises some 1,200 languages' is not the end of the main clause here, so a colon breaks the flow incorrectly. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘languages’: 'including the Nauruan and Marshallese' is a prepositional phrase that directly modifies 'languages'; no punctuation should separate a noun from its modifier. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘languages,’: a comma would suggest the phrase is a nonessential aside, but it is an integral part of describing the languages. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
